title: UseCase
description: Time-boxed delivery unit (Opportunity Management v2 delivery spine: Team/Project -> Epic -> Use-Case). A use-case is bound to exactly one product (productRef, N:1), implements one or more ideas (implementsIdeaRefs[], M:N, usually one), and is where real story points live. This is a lightweight entity that may optionally reference a formal UC-<COMPONENT>-<NNN> document via ucDocRef (per #015 open question 2). Following the linking principle, the volatile use-case points to stable product/idea assets; they never point back.

tenantId.description: Reserved for the future multi-tenant SaaS layer; null in the local file-based store.

workspaceId.description: Reserved workspace scope for the future SaaS layer; null in the local store.

slug.description: Filesystem-friendly slug, also the '<slug>.usecase.json' filename stem.

actor.description: Primary actor / role the use-case serves.

goal.description: Goal the actor achieves through this use-case.

storyPoints.description: Rollup story points = the latest estimation for this use-case (see estimation.schema.json). Null until estimated.

productRef.description: Id of the product (product.schema.json) this use-case is bound to (N:1). Required: a use-case cannot be estimated without a target product.

implementsIdeaRefs.description: Ids of the ideas (opportunity.schema.json) this use-case implements (M:N, usually one). Drives the GraphView 'implements' edge (use-case -> idea).

ucDocRef.description: Optional reference to a formal UC-<COMPONENT>-<NNN> use-case document (USE-CASE-METHODOLOGY). Null when the use-case is standalone.
